text.title: RAG Corpus Discovery Interview Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example: 7-SME audit of Faion support knowledge base before chunking design.

section.title: Worked example
section.p: Team: Faion ML-engineering. Task: scope support-bot RAG corpus before chunking design.
Step 1. Roster: 3 support leads + 1 policy author + 1 knowledge manager + 1 legal counsel + 1 SRE. All signed consent at `s3://faion-legal/consent/2026q2/`.

Step 2. Guide (5 prompts, past-behaviour anchored, non-leading): "Walk me through the last time you looked up a customer policy", "Show me where the answer actually lives", "Tell me about a time the answer was wrong or stale", "Describe how you decide which source to trust", "Which document classes are licensed for embedding?".
Step 3. 7 interviews recorded at s3://faion-research/2026q2/t{1..7}.m4a. Transcripts generated via Whisper batch.

Step 4. 23 quotes extracted, each attributed to transcript_id + timestamp. Examples:
t1@00:08:42: "I always check the Confluence policy first, then Slack #policy."
t4@00:21:33: "Customer contracts are not licensed for indexing."
t6@00:14:07: "GDPR class C data cannot be embedded outside EU region."

Step 5. Findings: f1 "Confluence policy is the authoritative source" → `finding` (4 transcripts support); f2 "Half FAQ entries are stale" → `hypothesis` (1 transcript only); f3 "Customer contracts cannot be embedded" → `finding` (legal counsel + 2 supports confirm).
Result. Report committed; downstream rag-bench spec drops customer-contract corpus from index, biases retrievers toward Confluence sources, opens FAQ-refresh ticket.
